item.title: Summer is here, the beach is calling, and my favorite Sony Bluetooth speaker is a must-have at less than $80
item.description: The Sony ULT Field 1 is one of my all-time favorite purchases, and you can snag one in time for your next summer get-together for less than $80.
item.author: derrek.lee@futurenet.com (Derrek Lee)
item.dc:creator: Derrek Lee

media:content.media:description: Sony ULT Field 1
media:content.media:text: Sony ULT Field 1
media:content.media:title: Sony ULT Field 1

item.content:encoded:
I live in Seattle, so when the sun's out, I tend to want to take advantage of it. Fortunately, I live next to a beach, and my friends and I love to go whenever we have a free moment. One thing I always take with me during gatherings like this is my Sony Ult Field 1, a small portable Bluetooth speaker with big, booming sound. I always highlight this product during major sales, and Prime Day has discounted it once again, bringing it down to its lowest price.

The Sony Ult Field 1 launched at a fairly reasonable $139.99, which falls in line with many portable Bluetooth speakers. It has gone on sale multiple times, but this most recent 44% discount takes nearly half off, bringing it down to just $78.

  • Prime Day returns June 23-26: see the full list of deals

At the original $139.99, the Sony ULT Field 1 was already a pretty good deal, but for under $80, this small but loud Bluetooth speaker is an absolute steal, especially given that it's one of our favorite speakers.View Deal

✅Recommended if: You want a loud speaker with plenty of bass that's small enough to carry around and has a ton of battery.

❌Skip this deal if: You want a stereo audio experience without needing a second speaker.

I purchased the Sony ULT Field 1 over a year ago, and it's been one of the best Black Friday purchases I've made. Since then, it's been a staple of every beach day or meetup I've gone to. While it's small and easy to carry, the Sony ULT Field 1 is surprisingly loud, producing great sound for any occasion and audible from quite a distance.

That's before you even get to the ULT button. The Field 1 already has decent bass, but when you press the ULT button, the speaker takes it to the next level with the help of the side passive radiators. And while there's no stereo sound on the one speaker, you can pair it with a second Ult Field speaker for a stereo experience, and at this price, you might as well get two.

You can also be sure that it'll last long enough to keep the party going, thanks to its 12-hour battery life. And because it's IP67 water- and dust-resistant, you can take it with you on any adventure.

That said, if you want something a little more powerful, the Sony ULT Field 3, which features enhanced audio with a 7-band equalizer, 24-hour battery life, fast charging, and the ability to link up to 100 compatible speakers for a true party experience. And with all that battery, it also acts as a portable power bank with a two-way USB-C port, which can be helpful when you're out and about.

You can grab this powerful portable speaker for less than $150, a sweet $72 discount from its regular price. While we love the ULT Field 1, the Field 3 might be a better fit if you don't mind the splurge.View Deal

item.title: Beat the tech price surge: Samsung's flagship Galaxy Tab S11 is a compelling laptop alternative and it's DOWN BY $265
item.description: Not ready to spend $1,000 on a new laptop? Samsung's Galaxy Tab S11 is now just $535.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia

media:content.media:description: The Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 with the Book Cover Keyboard attached.
media:content.media:text: The Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 with the Book Cover Keyboard attached.
media:content.media:title: The Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 with the Book Cover Keyboard attached.

item.content:encoded:
We're living in a time where gadget prices seem to be rising everywhere, or at least heading that way. Thankfully, Amazon's Prime Day sale is bucking that trend, bringing Samsung's flagship Galaxy Tab S11 down by a substantial $265.

Originally priced at $800, the Galaxy Tab S11 is now available for just $535. That's the lowest price we've seen on Samsung's flagship tablet so far, and at this price, it starts looking like one of the more compelling tech deals of Prime Day 2026.

  • Prime Day is back June 23-26: see the full list of deals

Samsung's Galaxy Tab S11 is down to just $535 during Prime Day, saving you $265 off its usual price. With an AMOLED display, bundled S Pen, flagship Dimensity 9400+ chipset, and DeX support, it's one of the best Android tablet deals right now.

Need more storage? It's worth mentioning that the 512GB configuration of the tablet is also 33% off during Amazon Prime Day, dropping the price of that version to $807.49.View Deal

✅Recommended if: you're looking for a premium Android tablet, want an AMOLED display, need an S Pen included in the box, or want a capable laptop alternative without spending laptop money.

❌Skip this deal if: you want a larger display, ultra-thin bezels, or prefer the software experience offered by other Android manufacturers.

One of the biggest parts of the Galaxy Tab S11 is its display. While many Android tablets in this price range still use IPS LCD panels, Samsung continues to offer an 11-inch AMOLED 2x screen. That means deeper blacks, richer colors, and a noticeably better experience for watching movies, TV shows, and YouTube videos.

Thankfully, Samsung didn't sacrifice performance either. The Galaxy Tab S11 is powered by MediaTek's flagship Dimensity 9400+ chipset, built on a 3nm process, and comes with up to 12GB of RAM and 512GB of storage. Being a flagship Galaxy device also means you get access to Samsung's latest Galaxy AI features, including conversational photo editing, Circle to Search, and AI-powered Writing Assist.

Why Galaxy Tab S11 makes more sense than buying a laptop right now

What makes this deal especially interesting is the timing. Laptop prices are facing pressure from rising memory and component costs, which means the same budget that once bought a great laptop often only gets you something mid-range today.

The Galaxy Tab S11 offers an alternative. Pair it with the right accessories, and it can easily become a portable laptop-style setup. The included S Pen is also a major bonus, especially for students, note-taking, creative work, or general productivity.

And with Samsung DeX continuing to improve, Android tablets are more capable than they've ever been. The Galaxy Tab S11 can provide a surprisingly computer-like experience while costing significantly less than many premium laptops.

Add in the excellent AMOLED display, and it's a package that's genuinely hard to ignore at $535.

item.title: Galaxy Watch 9 couldn't wait: major leak has me looking forward to the summer
item.description: The Galaxy Watch 9 finds itself wrapped in a major leak that highlights its design and more.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz

media:content.media:description: The Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 on a railing.
media:content.media:text: The Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 on a railing.
media:content.media:title: The Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 on a railing.

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • A leak claims to have discovered the alleged design for Samsung's Galaxy Watch 9.
  • The device, reportedly featuring a 40mm and 44mm variant, could feature a similar design to the Watch 8, but with a slightly altered default band.
  • Samsung confirmed during its huge Health app upgrade that many new features will be present in the Watch 9, while also stating the Snapdragon Wear Elite will power it.

We're expecting Samsung to release another wave of smartwatches this summer, and that's where this major leak leads us.

Alongside tipster OnLeaks, Android Headlines claims the Galaxy Watch 9 could feature a similar design to the Watch 8 from 2025. The tipster was credited with several leaked renders, showcasing a slightly refreshed smartwatch design. "Squircle" was a buzzword that accompanied many Galaxy Watch 8, and those continue for the 9. The alleged renderings show the circular watch face atop a brushed metal body. The watch appears thin, but it's not clear if Samsung's managed to shave down anymore.

Android Central's Take

Don't think I mind the continued design (if this rumor's true). I will say that the Pixel Watch is a gorgeous device. A clean glass-dome design that's uniquely Google. Samsung's new approach with the Galaxy Watch 8 and that squircle design is clean, too. It's a comfortable watch and that's easy on the eyes. It's not overly gaudy or trying to do too much. For me, it makes sense that Samsung would think to stick with it.

Rumors suggest the Watch 9 will be available in two sizes: 40mm and 44mm. A closer look at the watch's display purports screen bezels similar to what we already have. Two physical buttons sit on the right side of the watch, as well. The publication adds that the Galaxy Watch 9 will feature Bluetooth and LTE connectivity.

While the watch seems mostly the same, the renders show the device with a slightly altered band.

A subtle divot rolls down the center of the band like a racing stripe, whereas the Galaxy Watch 8's default band is smooth with a soft, bumpy underside. Two colorways are expected for each watch size, beginning with "Cream" and "Graphite" for the 40mm and "Silver" and "Graphite" for the 44mm variant. Price increases were rumored at ~$50 more for this year's models.

Major internal changes

Galaxy Watch 9 rumors have been surfacing slowly, but a recent report has us paying attention. Samsung's next watches, alongside the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, were supposedly spotted receiving 3C certification in China for charging. It appears that Samsung will equip the watches with 10W wired charging support, similar to what we have with the Watch 8. Battery sizes weren't specified. It's unlikely to see them at 3C, anyway.

Aside from rumors and leaks, Samsung confirmed that there will be new health features available for its Galaxy Watch 9 with its Health app overhaul. The company kicked off its Health app update on June 8, introducing a new Vitals feature, incorporating Daily Cardio Load, Heart Health Score, and more. A Heart Health Score is also present to note how your sleep, stress, and activity affect you. Many of these new features and the updated Antioxidant and AGEs Index will be available on the Watch 9.

Moreover, Samsung's going back to Qualcomm. The next Galaxy Watch is confirmed to feature the Snapdragon Wear Elite, boosting the device in multiple ways.

item.title: What the! This Meta Quest gift card deal is like a money duplication glitch in real life!
item.description: Meta Quest gamers are getting the ultimate Prime Day hack with this wild gift card discount.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich

media:content.media:description: Grabbing a Meta Quest 3 controller that has AMVR grips attached
media:content.media:text: Grabbing a Meta Quest 3 controller that has AMVR grips attached
media:content.media:title: Grabbing a Meta Quest 3 controller that has AMVR grips attached

item.content:encoded:
Getting a great discount on Prime Day is fun, but there's nothing like getting free money on Prime Day instead. Amazon and Meta have discounted Meta Quest gift cards, quite literally giving you free money when you buy one.

Meta Quest gift cards can be used to buy anything on the Meta Quest store, including games, apps, and in-app purchases. That means these are the perfect gift for anyone you know who plays VR games on a Quest, as they can grab any game they want or expand their in-game inventory in titles like Gorilla Tag, Ug, Animal Company, Scary Baboon, and more.

$25 gift card: $25 $21.50 at Amazon

$50 gift card: $50 $42.50 at Amazon

$75 gift card: $75 $63.75 at Amazon

$100 gift card: $100 $85 at AmazonView Deal

✅Recommended if: You want to get great games at a discount rate, or you're looking to buy a gift for a friend or loved one who is a VR gamer.

❌Skip this deal if: You want a physical gift card. These are digital gift cards only.

My son is obsessed with the idea of glitching out games to duplicate items, and it's always fun to see when companies lean into that obsession with real rewards. In Animal Company, you can duplicate items using a special machine, but on Prime Day, Meta Quest gift cards let you do the money glitch in real life.

Since these gift cards are digital, you won't even have to wait to get them! That means you can pick up great new titles right away, or just stockpile virtual gift cards until the time is right. They also make the best gifts, whether it's for an upcoming birthday, a random summer celebration, or even just to grab some (very) early Christmas presents.

item.description: Amazon is slashing up to 45% off some of its best Kindle e-readers, including the Paperwhite, Scribe, and Colorsoft.
item.author: patrick.farmer@futurenet.com (Patrick Farmer)
item.dc:creator: Patrick Farmer

media:content.media:description: The Notebook tab showing various notes on the Amazon Kindle Scribe
media:content.media:text: The Notebook tab showing various notes on the Amazon Kindle Scribe
media:content.media:title: The Notebook tab showing various notes on the Amazon Kindle Scribe

item.content:encoded:
Get ready, folks: Prime Day 2026 has arrived, and this year Amazon is showcasing some particularly impressive discounts on Kindle e-readers. Whether you want the budget-friendly base model or feature-packed Colorsoft Signature Edition, I've dropped a selection of my favorite Prime Day Kindle deals into this guide.

These versatile devices give you the ability to carry thousands of books with you wherever you go, plus you'll enjoy great battery life, adjustable displays, and even AI-boosted software features with certain models. Some of the Prime Day deals listed below also come with accessories like stands, chargers, and protective sleeves, so take a look and get ready to crush those 2026 reading goals.

Just keep in mind that most of the best e-reader deals will only be available to Prime members. Haven't signed up yet? Check out Amazon's 30-day free trial to enjoy all of the Prime Day festivities without paying a cent.

When is Prime Day?

This year's Amazon Prime Day is set to run from June 23rd through June 26th. The four-day sale event kicked off on Tuesday at 12:01am PDT and will end on Friday at 11:59pm PDT.

Do I need to be a Prime member to shop during Prime Day?

The vast majority of Prime Day deals are only be available to Amazon Prime members. If you haven't joined yet, it's worth mentioning that customers who sign up for Amazon's 30-day free trial will get access to all of the exclusive deals without paying a dime, plus you'll get other membership benefits, such as free shipping, unlimited streaming, fuel discounts, and more.

Which Kindle e-reader is best?

We consider the Kindle Paperwhite (12th Gen) to be the overall best Kindle e-reader for most people, thanks to its sophisticated blend of specs, value, and useful features. The device boasts up to three months of battery life on a single charge, plus you get a highly-adjustable 300ppi E Ink display, 16GB of storage, and IPX8 water resistance.

item.title: Xreal's best smart glasses are up to $100 off for Prime Day, and they're my absolute favorite way to game on the go
item.description: Xreal's wearable TV smart glasses are the best in the business, and now they're at the best price thanks to Prime Day.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich

media:content.media:description: Wearing a pair of Xreal One Pro smart glasses
media:content.media:text: Wearing a pair of Xreal One Pro smart glasses
media:content.media:title: Wearing a pair of Xreal One Pro smart glasses

item.content:encoded:
My flight home from New York this week got delayed several hours, but I passed the time by playing 007 First Light on a giant home theater in the airplane. No, I don't have my own private plane, but Xreal 1S certainly helps make it feel that way, and now you can get your very own portable, wearable home theater for $50 off with this fantastic Prime Day deal.

Xreal 1S is a pair of smart display glasses, also known as a wearable TV or home theater, that packs dual micro-OLED panels into a svelte pair of glasses. Plug the USB-C cable into any compatible device — be it a laptop, Steam Deck, ROG Ally, or even your phone — and you'll be greeted with a massive floating display in front of your face.

Xreal set itself apart from the pack when it launched the Xreal One series last year, and this year's Xreal 1S upgrades things with even better displays and the amazing X1 chip inside that provides full 3DoF spatial tracking for the virtual display. In short, that means the display can smoothly follow your head movements to iron out airplane turbulence, or be "pinned" to a wall in your home to create the ultimate virtual home theater experience.

Get the home theater that travels with you! With a giant virtual screen that's fully spatially tracked, you'll be chilling in comfort while watching movies or playing games on your favorite devices, no matter where you find yourself.

Price comparison: Best Buy - $399View Deal

✅Recommended if: You're tired of boring phones and want something new and interesting, want a phone with a solid camera and lots of great updates, or you just don't want to spend a ton of money on a new phone.

❌Skip this deal if: You want glasses with a wider FoV, or if the reflections from the underside of Birdbath lenses annoy you. They also aren't compatible with a Nintendo Switch or Switch 2.

Xreal 1S is a full replacement for Xreal One, as it ships with brighter, higher-resolution micro-OLED panels and brings down the weight by 2g in the process. Plus, it's got a great blue color that looks snazzy as heck.

Just keep in mind that it isn't compatible with the Nintendo Switch or Switch 2. If you were planning on using them with any of Nintendo's handhelds, you'll need to pick up a pair of Viture smart glasses and the Viture Pro Mobile Dock, instead.

Xreal One Pro upgrades the Xreal 1S experience with prism lenses, a slimmer profile, wider FoV, and all the great automatic 3D and spatial tracking features Xreal fans know and love.

Price comparison: Best Buy - $549View Deal

✅Recommended if: You want a top-tier wearable home theater experience, the best prism lenses on the market, or want smart glasses that support 6DoF tracking (with the optional Xreal Eye accessory).

❌Skip this deal if: You want to use these on a Nintendo Switch or Switch 2. They're just not compatible.

Xreal also has an upgraded pair called Xreal One Pro, which further increases display quality with prism lenses. These lenses are a completely different design from the Birdbath style used on other smart glasses (including Xreal One and 1S), and offer an even clearer picture with a 5-degree wider FoV. That means you can see more of that virtual screen on Xreal One Pro than with other glasses.

Prism lenses also avoid the reflective nature of Birdbath lenses, so you won't see a slight reflection of your lap (or light colored clothing) on the display. That alone might be a big enough upgrade for some folks, and things get even cooler when you pair an Xreal Eye camera or Xreal Beam Pro with either of these glasses.

item.title: Nothing casually made a new phone: Phone 4b sketch teaser precedes a July reveal
item.description: Nothing officially teased the Phone 4b, and its clean sketch teaser shows off its design early.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz

media:content.media:description: Nothing Phone 4a vs Phone 4a Pro from the launch in London
media:content.media:text: Nothing Phone 4a vs Phone 4a Pro from the launch in London
media:content.media:title: Nothing Phone 4a vs Phone 4a Pro from the launch in London

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Nothing drops a Phone 4b sketch teaser, stating the phone will be revealed on July 3.
  • The device features a new take on its camera bar design with a vertical camera array and what looks like Nothing's Glyph Bar.
  • The company's co-founder says the Phone 4b is a "continuation of our naming system" with its own distinct voice.

Nothing's walking us through the alphabet this week with a major phone teaser that has us entering a new series.

Early this morning (June 23), Nothing continued its streak of teasers for a new product, and this one is the most substantial we've gotten yet. The company officially teased the Phone 4b, set to debut July 3. True to Nothing's brand and sense of humor, the post states, "Kept sketching the Phone (4a) series and accidentally made a new phone." The post on X features a cropped schematic with various sketches of the Phone 4a series that we know.

The top left features the Phone 4a, while the bottom, sliding into view, is the Phone 4a Pro. However, the center of the sketchboard offers a glimpse at a phone's camera bar we've yet to see. From what's been shown, it looks like the Nothing Phone 4b features a vertical camera system, which could hold two lenses. Beside it appears to be its LED flash.

The bottom of the camera bar seems to host the phone's Glyph Bar, which is the company's new take on the Glyph Matrix or Lighting system. Giving us more to think about is Nothing's co-founder, Akis Evangelidis. In a recent post, Evangelidis says, "What does (b) stand for? Nothing." He says this isn't some major departure from what consumers are accustomed to, but rather a "continuation of our naming system." This new Phone 4b will build upon what we've received through Nothing's a-series, but with its own clear voice.

Evangelidis adds that the A series will continue to be Nothing's "most premium line" below its flagship segment. It's not clear how the Phone 4b will line up price-wise, but if the A-series is premium below the flagships, then perhaps this next device sits under both.

4b or not 4...

Nothing's been teasing this new product on its account for India for a few days now. Things started with a couple of Pokémon images, which is textbook Nothing, as it often teases new devices with the powerful monsters. Nothing used images of Jumpluff and Blastoise. That wasn't the kicker. Nothing also announced the Community Review Program, aimed at bringing in consumers passionate about its brand and devices to get devices early for feedback.

In that post, Nothing featured a photo of Jumpluff, the same Pokémon it used to begin its new streak of teasers. We now know that Nothing's been teasing the Phone 4b; however, on its India account, what got the teasers started was Blastoise. There's a chance that Jumpluff is code for another device entirely. July 3 is when we'll see the Phone 4b revealed and have all of our answers.

Android Central's Take

Nothing really wants to have a hand across every price point for consumers, don't they? If you consider the Phone 3 is its flagship, then the Phone 4a series is its "premium line" under that, which settles somewhere around mid-range. What will the Phone 4b satisfy? I'm curious. I'm always pretty curious about what Nothing does. Its designs are always different, and it generally makes pretty interesting phones to begin with. July 3; remember it.

item.title: 3 tech products I used to help me lose more than 10 pounds in one month, just in time for summer (and they're all on sale right now!)
item.description: These tech products have helped me get back in shape in time for summer by keeping me motivated. Hopefully, these Prime Day discounts can motivate you, as well!
item.author: derrek.lee@futurenet.com (Derrek Lee)
item.dc:creator: Derrek Lee

media:content.media:description: A Pixel 10, Pixel Watch 4, and Oura Ring 4 on a Withings smart scale
media:content.media:text: A Pixel 10, Pixel Watch 4, and Oura Ring 4 on a Withings smart scale
media:content.media:title: A Pixel 10, Pixel Watch 4, and Oura Ring 4 on a Withings smart scale

item.content:encoded:
I've been on a weight loss journey for the past few months, and it's been going surprisingly well. While I was aiming to lose roughly 1 pound per week, I managed to lose 11 pounds in a single month, and I also decreased my body fat percentage and increased my muscle mass (my main goal).

These are the three main products that have helped me get into shape in time for the summer, all of which are on sale right now for Prime Day.

  • Prime Day returns June 23-26: see the full list of deals

Google Pixel Watch 4

The Pixel Watch 4 is one of my go-to smartwatches, thanks to its advanced health and fitness tracking, accurate GPS, and access to my favorite music and fitness apps, including YouTube Music, MyFitnessPal, and Strava.

With the Google Health app Premium subscription, I tell the AI health coach my goals, and it creates a fitness plan for me. Based on my goals, the coach put together a plan that incorporates some of what I already do and includes recommended gym and running sessions. I can then send them to my Pixel Watch 4 to track my workouts.

I can even use my Pixel Watch 4 as a live heart rate monitor for my Peloton, which adds even more value.

The Pixel Watch 4 is one of the best smartwatches you can buy, and it drops to its lowest price yet at just under $300 for the 41mm Wi-Fi variant.View Deal

Oura Ring 4

The Oura Ring 4 remains one of the best health and fitness wearables, one that I rarely took off my finger. My favorite thing about the Oura Ring 4 is that it has some of the best automatic activity detection on a wearable. It knows when I'm walking, running, working out, and even when I'm dancing, all without me having to manually track it.

The Oura Ring 4 is also incredibly comfortable to wear while sleeping, which is an important part of fitness.

Even with the newer Oura Ring 5 already on store shelves, the Oura Ring 4 is still worth it. The best part is that you can purchase the Oura Ring 4 and the Oura Membership with your FSA/HSA.

The Oura Ring 4 remains one of the best health wearables on the market, and it's now cheaper than ever for Prime Day, knocking off more than $100 and reaching its lowest price since launch.View Deal

Withings smart scale

It wasn't until after getting a Withings smart scale that I realized my weight trend had been going upwards over the past several years. Fortunately, that was a big part of my motivation to take my fitness and nutrition seriously.

Since I started paying more attention to what I eat and being more consistent with my workouts, I noticed that my weight trend had been going down, just as I expected. My Withings scale has become one of the main ways I track my progress, and while I don't expect it to be 100% accurate, I know it's at least in the ballpark for my weight, body fat, and muscle mass.

You don't have to spend hundreds of dollars on a smart body composition scale when you can spend less than $100 on the Withings Body Smart, one of our favorite smart scales.View Deal

Bonus: MyFitnessPal

MyFitnessPal is a great way to track your caloric intake and your macros. It's easy to log food in the app, and even on my Pixel Watch 4. With the subscription, you can make it easier by scanning barcodes. There's even an Intermittent Fasting mode to help you stay within your eating window, which I highly recommend.
